DISCOVERY 301, RE: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO CONDUCT FINANCIAL DISCOVERY ON DEFENDANTS PAUL HEUBERGER, AND SUZANNE NGUYEN HEUBERGER. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO CONDUCT FINANCIAL CONDITION DISCOVERY IS DENIED. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. A PROPOSED ORDER HAS BEEN SUBMITTED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs' Motion For Leave To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendants Paul Heuberger, And Suzanne Nguyen Heuberger
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS CHRISTOPHER JOHNSON, and MELANIE JOHNSON'S Motion For Leave To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendants Paul Heuberger, And Suzanne Nguyen Heuberger.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for leave to conduct financial condition discovery is denied. Discovery into a defendant's financial condition is subject to Civil Code, Sec. 3295, which prohibits such discovery absent court order. "[B]efore a court may enter an order permitting discovery of a defendant's financial condition, it must (1) weigh the evidence submitted in favor of and in opposition to motion for discovery, and (2) make a finding that it is very likely the plaintiff will prevail on his claim for punitive damages." (Jabro v. Superior Court (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 754, 758.) "Very likely" means more than a reasonable probability, and more than a prima facie showing of the kind needed to defeat summary judgment. (Id.) The court concludes, based on a review of all of the evidence presented by the parties on this motion, that plaintiffs have not met their burden of showing it is very likely they will prevail on a claim for punitive damages. In particular, defendants present evidence that they relied on the advice of counsel in their dealings with plaintiffs.

A proposed order has been submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
